pi_items.source: Code analysis: task.json metadata, setup_task.sh (line 31 cleanup search), export_result.sh (line 64 event search), verifier.py (line 44 validation check). All scripts expect exactly this event name.

Code analysis: setup_data.py line 46 creates contact with name 'Grace Patel' and job_position 'CFO'. Verifier.py line 53-55 checks for 'Grace Patel' in attendees_found list. export_result.sh line 56 queries 'Grace Patel' by name.

Code analysis: setup_data.py line 45 creates contact with name 'Frank Rivera' and job_position 'HR Business Partner'. Verifier.py line 59-61 checks for 'Frank Rivera' in attendees_found list. export_result.sh line 56 queries 'Frank Rivera' by name.
Code analysis: verifier.py line 68 checks if location field contains 'Executive Boardroom'. Task description (task.json line 5) specifies location should be 'Executive Boardroom'.

Code analysis: verifier.py lines 77-82 explicitly checks for keywords ['q2', 'performance', 'retention'] in lowercase description. Task description (task.json line 5) specifies description should mention 'Review Q2 department performance outcomes and discuss talent retention strategy'.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'schedule_from_contact@1' requires an agent to navigate to the Contacts module in a local Odoo instance, find contact Grace Patel (CFO at fictional company northbridge.org), and schedule a meeting named 'Q2 Performance Debrief with CFO'. The meeting must include two attendees: Grace Patel and Frank Rivera (HR Business Partner), be scheduled for Next Thursday at 14:00 (2:00 PM), be located at 'Executive Boardroom', and include a description containing the keywords 'Q2', 'performance', and 'retention'. All data is synthetically generated in a Docker-based test environment with predefined contacts and calendar structure.
